title: Mode A — Fresh state file, HubSpot live, all phases pending
description: Standard status check on first run. No state file exists yet. HubSpot returns pipeline data. Digest posted to Slack.
notes: phase-state-reader initializes 5-phase empty structure in memory only. No disk write occurs without an explicit advance operation. pipeline-review entry criterion 'HubSpot pipeline data accessible' should be marked met: true.

title: Mode A — HubSpot unavailable; pipeline-review criteria unevaluable
description: Status check when HubSpot is offline. pipeline-review entry and exit criteria that require HubSpot data are marked unevaluable, not failed. Digest still posts.
notes: HubSpot unavailability does NOT trigger a hard stop for this orchestrator (unlike deal-desk-watcher). The pipeline-review phase criteria that depend on HubSpot are marked unevaluable: true with reason 'HubSpot unavailable'. The digest header shows 'Data as of: unavailable — HubSpot offline'. The G6 data freshness line is never omitted.

title: Mode B — Advance pipeline-review from pending to in_progress
description: Operator opens the first phase. Entry criteria are met (first cycle). phase-gate-writer is invoked with require_confirmation: true. Operator confirms. State file is written.
notes: This is the first write to the state file in the cycle lifecycle. The state file is created (or written for the first time) by phase-gate-writer only after operator confirmation. The transition record in the phase's transitions array includes transitioned_at ISO timestamp and approved_by: 'operator'.

title: Mode B — Sequence violation: attempt to open quota-setting before pipeline-review is complete
description: Operator requests advancing quota-setting while pipeline-review is still in_progress. The orchestrator detects the violation and reports it without invoking phase-gate-writer.

expected_behavior.violation_message_contains: pipeline-review must be complete before quota-setting can open

notes: The orchestrator evaluates sequence rules BEFORE invoking phase-gate-writer. A violation means phase-gate-writer is never called. The cycle-digest-poster is still invoked so the operator sees the current state board. The write_summary passed to cycle-digest-poster is { status: 'not_triggered', operation_performed: null }.

title: Mode B — Mark pipeline-review complete; quota-setting entry criteria now met
description: Operator marks pipeline-review complete. phase-gate-writer appends the complete transition and sets completed_date. The resulting digest shows quota-setting as 'ready to open'.
notes: Exit criteria on pipeline-review are marked met: true ONLY because the phase status was explicitly transitioned to complete by the operator — not because HubSpot data suggested the criteria were satisfied. This is the core integrity constraint of the exit criteria system.

title: Mode C — Add blocker to quota-setting
description: Operator adds a blocker to the quota-setting phase while it is in_progress. phase-gate-writer appends the blocker, sets phase status to blocked, and appends a transition record.

input.operator_command: add blocker to quota-setting: Finance approval for Q3 quota bands pending — Sarah Chen OOO until May 20

notes: G8 enforcement: the digest blocked-phase detail block must show the blocker description, raised_by, and raised_at. The blocker_id is generated by phase-gate-writer using the pattern blk-[phase_id_slug]-[epoch_seconds].

title: Mode C — Resolve blocker; phase returns to in_progress
description: Operator resolves the single blocker on quota-setting. Since all blockers are now resolved, the phase status returns to in_progress and a transition record is appended.

existing_blockers.description: Finance approval for Q3 quota bands pending — Sarah Chen OOO until May 20

notes: The status only returns to in_progress if ALL blockers have resolved_at set. A partial resolution (multiple blockers, one resolved) leaves the phase blocked. The resolved_at is set on the individual blocker, not removed from the array — the blocker record persists for audit.

title: Hard stop — company_name missing from company profile
description: Company profile exists but company_name field is absent. Orchestrator stops immediately, reports the missing field, and invokes no subagents.

expected_behavior.error_message_contains: Required company profile field missing: company_name

notes: This is the earliest possible hard stop — before any subagent is invoked. The orchestrator reports 'Run rev-ops cold-start to configure.' as the recovery hint. No state file is read, no HubSpot is contacted.

title: Hard stop — cycle state file corrupt (invalid JSON)
description: The state file at cycle_state_path exists but cannot be parsed. phase-state-reader returns { error: 'state_file_corrupt' }. Orchestrator stops and reports the corruption without invoking phase-gate-writer or cycle-digest-poster.

expected_behavior.recovery_hint_contains: Back up the file and delete it to reset

notes: The hard stop occurs when phase-state-reader returns { error: 'state_file_corrupt' }. The orchestrator surfaces the corruption, gives the recovery path, and exits cleanly. A MISSING state file is not corruption — it initializes the empty cycle structure in memory.

title: Mode B — Operator declines gate write (skip); digest still posts
description: Operator requests marking quota-setting complete but cancels at the confirmation prompt. write_status is skipped. cycle-digest-poster is invoked with the pre-write state and reports the skip in the Gate Write section.

expected_behavior.digest_gate_write_section: ⏭️  Operator skipped gate write — no state changes made

notes: Operator declining is not an error — it is a valid outcome. The orchestrator continues to cycle-digest-poster with the pre-write phases array. The digest accurately reflects the unchanged state. The write_log from phase-gate-writer records human_approval_status: 'skipped'.
